name: Marvin Hamlisch
abstract: Marvin Frederick Hamlisch (June 2, 1944 – August 6, 2012) was an American composer and conductor. He is one of a handful of people to win Emmy, Grammy, Oscar, and Tony awards, a feat dubbed the "EGOT".

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: Therefore Marvin Hamlisch, in order to use the music in the film, had to use my editions. All that he could do – he could slightly rearrange them... He did a really remarkable job of fitting that music to the story.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I had to pay $300,000 to get them back on “Sanford and Son,” but I got it. ... I gave Marvin Hamlisch his too, because it’s a legacy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: That was another good team to work with, with Quincy Jones, Phil Moore, Luther Henderson, Marvin Hamlisch.
